channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Education Minister directs investigation into alleged Cambridge exam paper leaks
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Federal Minister for Education and Professional Training, Khalid Maqbool Siddiqui, has taken serious notice of fresh claims regarding leaks of Cambridge examination papers in Pakistan. The ...

item.title: Saskatoon Mamba Begin First Season After Rebranding
item.description: The Saskatoon Mamba are set to begin their first regular season following a full rebranding, transitioning from their former identity as the Saskatoon Rattlers. The change encompasses not only the tea...

item.title: Abia State Begins Feasibility Study For Azumini-Obeaku Seaport Project
item.description: Abia State Government don give green light make dem start feasibility study for di proposed Azumini-Obeaku Seaport and Inland Waterways Corridor project. Governor Alex Otti announce dis approval durin...

item.title: 1960 Encounter Between Filipino Fisherman and USS Triton During Secret Submarine Voyage
item.description: On April 1, 1960, 19-year-old Filipino fisherman Rufino Baring had an extraordinary encounter with the USS Triton (SSRN-586), a nuclear-powered submarine of the U.S. Navy. The Triton was undertaking O...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel discusses Melania Trump joke and White House backlash with fellow late night hosts
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel recently opened up about the fallout from a controversial joke he made about First Lady Melania Trump, describing her as having 'a glow like an expected widow' on his show. The comment, m...

item.title: Father Mourns Slain JOOST Student Alice Rianga as Suspect is Arrested
item.description: In Bondo, Kenya, the father of 20-year-old JOOST student Alice Rianga, Richard Rianga, has opened up about the painful loss of his daughter. Alice had been reported missing for several days before her...

item.title: The Ethical Risks of AI Chatbots and Personalized Persuasion
item.description: Generative AI chatbots have transformed the landscape of persuasion by delivering highly personalized messages that can exploit users' emotions, preferences, and vulnerabilities. Unlike traditional ad...

item.title: India Faces Scrap Steel Supply Constraints While Targeting Higher Recycling in Steel Sector
item.description: India, the world’s second largest steel producer, is aiming to double the use of scrap in steel production by 2047. The government envisions scrap feeding 50% of steel output, up from the current 23%,...

item.title: Pakistan records lowest fiscal deficit in nearly three decades due to strong fuel levy and provincial surpluses
item.description: ISLAMABAD – Pakistan has achieved its lowest fiscal deficit in around 27 years, reaching just 0.7 per cent of GDP in the first three quarters of the current fiscal year. This outcome is mainly attribu...

item.title: ConCourt confirms Home Affairs can block repeat asylum applications
item.description: The Constitutional Court of South Africa has delivered a ruling confirming that asylum seekers whose initial claims have been rejected cannot submit repeat applications. This decision overturns a prev...

item.title: Railway employee honoured posthumously after foiling suicide attack in Attock
item.description: In a deeply moving incident from Attock district, Muhammad Liaqat Ali, a railway employee, sacrificed his life while preventing a suspected suicide attack near a police checkpost in Jand tehsil, close...

item.title: Short-Term Diet Adjustments May Reduce Biological Age in Older Adults
item.description: A recent study from the University of Sydney suggests that short-term dietary changes can influence biological aging in adults aged 65 to 75. In a four-week trial involving 104 participants, researche...

item.title: ACT Government welcomes federal budget with key infrastructure and community funding
item.description: The ACT Government has welcomed the 2026-27 federal budget as a positive shift towards fairer treatment of Canberra, despite limited new funding for major projects. Chief Minister Andrew Barr and Trea...

item.title: Nyakanazi Power Expansion Project Progress at 49 Percent Causes Concern for Energy Ministry
item.description: The Nyakanazi Power expansion project, aimed at increasing electricity supply in Tanzania, has reached only 49 percent completion, causing concern for the Ministry of Energy. The project, which is cru...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa Returns to Senate After Months Away Due to ICC Warrant
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa recently returned to the Philippine Senate after a six-month absence, citing personal shame and pressing legislative duties as reasons for his comeback. His absence beg...

item.title: James Comer avoids answering questions about Trump's statements on Americans' financial concerns
item.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer (R-KY) faced reporters regarding President Donald Trump's recent comments about the rising cost-of-living crisis. When asked by Meidas Touch reporter Pab...

item.title: Dela Rosa's Lawyers Prepare Legal Action Against NBI Agents Over Arrest Attempt
item.description: The legal team representing former PNP Chief Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa is preparing to file complaints against certain National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) agents involved in a widely circulated arres...

item.title: Limpopo Police Hunt Suspect After Correctional Officer Fatally Shot in Modimolle
item.description: In a tragic incident in Modimolle, Limpopo, a female correctional officer was shot and killed on Tuesday morning while walking to work along Nelson Mandela Road. Police spokesperson Col Malesela Ledwa...

item.title: Israel Qualifies for Eurovision Final Amid Audience Protests and Sound Difficulties; Boy George Exits
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest returned on 12 May 2026 in Vienna with the first live semi-final, featuring 15 performers competing for a place in Saturday's grand finale. Israeli singer Noam Bettan quali...

item.title: Texas man uncovers possible serial killer after decades-long investigation into daughter’s disappearance
item.description: Tim Miller, a Texan whose teenage daughter Laura disappeared in 1984 and was later found murdered, spent decades trying to find her killer. Frustrated by what he considered inadequate police investiga...
